body.title: TWM: ACTIVE_GOAL entry type — parsed_goal lands in working memory
body.result: ACTIVE_GOAL singleton in TWM via CognitiveMilieuChannel + cortex.twm_get_active_goal() + twm_evict_category(). Session 2026-04-02a.
body.description:
Currently parsed_goal only lives in the basket — it evaporates after the turn. TWM has no concept of 'what Igor is currently trying to accomplish.' This means TWM decay, cortex search, and habit scoring are all goal-blind.

Fix: when PARSE_GOAL fires and emits parsed_goal to basket, also emit it to TWM as an ACTIVE_GOAL entry. This is an EMITIF to the 'cognitive_milieu' or TWM channel in the PARSE_GOAL brick's code_ref tool.

Implementation:
1. Add ACTIVE_GOAL as a recognized TWM observation type (or reuse existing type with tag active_goal=True)
2. Extend PARSE_GOAL's code_ref tool (when seeded to live DB) to push parsed_goal → TWM as ACTIVE_GOAL, TTL=300s (5 min — goal context window)
3. ACTIVE_GOAL entries are singleton: seeding a new one replaces the old one (goal shift)
4. Expose via twm.get_active_goal() helper for use by decay scorer and SITUATE

Prerequisite for: T-twm-relevance-decay, SITUATE real implementation.
Design refs: Gemini threading analysis (RL_gemini_threading_20260401), D297 PARSE_GOAL basket contract.
